verdict_rationale: All MAJOR findings from the prior audit (God Module, Long Functions, capability-matrix drift, isinstance-on-FileLayout coupling, under-pinned per-tool capability tests) are closed; only two minor INFO residuals remain.
remediation_diff_summary: Phase 4a split the 721-line `agentic_tool_spec.py` into a 300-line data-model/registry module plus a `tool_specs/` package with one factory per tool (each 45-153 lines) and two shared factories (`_rules_factory.py`, `_mcp_server_factory.py`). Phase 2 introduced polymorphic `FileLayout` methods (`probe_check_path`, `tolerates_missing_config_key`), eliminating the `isinstance` switches in `tool_status.py`. Phase 1 added an `AgenticToolSpec.__post_init__` that rejects `config_dir_keys`/`io` key-set drift at construction. Tests now pin per-tool `map_key_path`, `file_format`, `key_field`, and round-trip `rules`, `mcp_server`, and `slash_command` cells for every tool.
detailed_analysis: Slice 05 has been fully reworked. CQ-01 (God Module): agentic_tool_spec.py is now 300 lines. CQ-02 (Long Functions): the five 100+-line builder functions are gone; per-tool factories range 46-153 lines, with declarative return AgenticToolSpec(...) literals and ~1 cyclomatic complexity. CQ-03 (capability-matrix drift): AgenticToolSpec.__post_init__ raises ValueError on config_dir_keys/io key drift; test pins both directions. CQ-04 (Data Clumps on McpServerDialect): mitigated structurally but dialect still typed Any; INFO. CQ-05 (Any|None on _mcp_server_io): persists as INFO — quoted forward reference would safely restore static checking. CQ-06 (unused-parameter closures): intrinsic to ParseFn protocol; INFO. CQ-07 (isinstance switch in ToolStatusTracker): closed by polymorphism — ensure_roots and _probe_tool_roots both call layout.probe_check_path/tolerates_missing_config_key. CQ-08 (asymmetric missing-config-key guard): closed via the predicate. CQ-09 (per-tool capability matrix under-pinned): closed by test_mcp_server_layout_per_tool parametrized over claude/codex/opencode. CQ-10 (round-trip coverage holes): closed by test_mcp_server_round_trip_per_tool + test_slash_command_round_trip_per_tool + test_rules_round_trip_per_tool. No new findings.

findings.category: W4 Data Clumps / W8 Primitive Obsession (mitigated)
findings.description: Per-tool MCP differences are still expressed as a bag of optional McpServerDialect kwargs rather than typed per-tool subclasses. Severity is materially reduced because each tool's dialect literal is now co-located with that tool's factory and test_mcp_server_layout_per_tool pins the load-bearing differences. The smell persists at the type level.
findings.recommendation: Replace `dialect: Any | None` with `'McpServerDialect | None'` forward reference; this would catch field-name typos via mypy without forcing a runtime import.

findings.description: `dialect: Any | None = None` on `build_mcp_server_io` keeps the public-shape factory weakly typed to dodge a runtime import. Since `from __future__ import annotations` is in effect, a quoted forward reference is safe.
findings.recommendation: Change to `dialect: 'McpServerDialect | None' = None` to restore mypy coverage at no runtime cost.

findings.description: Inner parse_* closures still accept artifact_path and artifact_root kwargs that several of them ignore. This is forced by the ParseFn protocol signature.
findings.recommendation: No structural fix recommended — noise is intrinsic to the protocol contract and now scoped to short per-tool closures.
